evidence_quote: Few known loci replicate with genome-wide significance or even marginally in the KhoeSan populations studied here. Four SNPs in the genes SLC45A2 (rs16891982, p = 1.2e-3), KITLG (rs12821256, p = 0.02), and SLC24A5 (rs1426654, p = 9.8e-9 and rs2470102, p = 1.1e-8) marginally replicate in the zKhomani + Nama under an additive model.
classification_notes: Paper's own STAR Methods text ('Replication of known pigmentation loci') explicitly names this SNP as one of only 4 SNPs that 'marginally replicate' — the paper's chosen word for even its strongest known-locus hits. S6A/Table2 reports p_joint=0.00116101 for this SNP in the imputed-genotype zKhomani+Nama replication panel (distinct from the larger targeted-resequencing analysis reported elsewhere in Results for SLC24A5, which used a different, larger sample and yields a more significant p-value for the same or a linked SNP — see notes on p_joint discrepancy for SLC24A5 rows).

evidence_quote: Few known loci replicate with genome-wide significance or even marginally in the KhoeSan populations studied here. Four SNPs in the genes SLC45A2 (rs16891982, p = 1.2e-3), KITLG (rs12821256, p = 0.02), and SLC24A5 (rs1426654, p = 9.8e-9 and rs2470102, p = 1.1e-8) marginally replicate in the zKhomani + Nama under an additive model.
classification_notes: Paper's own STAR Methods text ('Replication of known pigmentation loci') explicitly names this SNP as one of only 4 SNPs that 'marginally replicate' — the paper's chosen word for even its strongest known-locus hits. S6A/Table2 reports p_joint=9.76903e-09 for this SNP in the imputed-genotype zKhomani+Nama replication panel (distinct from the larger targeted-resequencing analysis reported elsewhere in Results for SLC24A5, which used a different, larger sample and yields a more significant p-value for the same or a linked SNP — see notes on p_joint discrepancy for SLC24A5 rows). Same resequencing-vs-replication-panel distinction applies: S6A/Table2 (imputed replication panel) reports p_joint=9.769030e-09 (paper text rounds to 9.8e-9); this matches the number the main text Novel Variants section also reports for the resequencing analysis (p=9.8e-9) — i.e. for rs1426654 specifically the two analyses converge on the same value, unlike rs2470102 above where they diverge.

evidence_quote: Few known loci replicate with genome-wide significance or even marginally in the KhoeSan populations studied here. Four SNPs in the genes SLC45A2 (rs16891982, p = 1.2e-3), KITLG (rs12821256, p = 0.02), and SLC24A5 (rs1426654, p = 9.8e-9 and rs2470102, p = 1.1e-8) marginally replicate in the zKhomani + Nama under an additive model.
classification_notes: Paper's own STAR Methods text ('Replication of known pigmentation loci') explicitly names this SNP as one of only 4 SNPs that 'marginally replicate' — the paper's chosen word for even its strongest known-locus hits. S6A/Table2 reports p_joint=1.05268e-08 for this SNP in the imputed-genotype zKhomani+Nama replication panel (distinct from the larger targeted-resequencing analysis reported elsewhere in Results for SLC24A5, which used a different, larger sample and yields a more significant p-value for the same or a linked SNP — see notes on p_joint discrepancy for SLC24A5 rows). IMPORTANT ANALYSIS-SOURCE DISTINCTION: the imputed-genotype replication panel (S6A/Table2, this row) reports p_joint=1.052680e-08 (paper text rounds to 1.1e-8). A SEPARATE, larger targeted-resequencing analysis (441 KhoeSan samples, described in main text p.1348-1349) reports a more significant p=3.6e-12 for the same rs2470102/SLC24A5 signal. Both are genome-wide significant by the 5e-8 threshold, but they are NOT the same statistical test/sample and should not be conflated when comparing to other papers' single reported p-value.

coord_build: GRCh37/hg19 (exact bp position not given in main text; region spans SMARCA2/VLDLR per Figure 5C, chr9:~2.0-2.7Mb per Table S5 VLDLR entry)
gws_threshold: p < 5e-8 (not met; this is one of >5 independent low-frequency (<5%) signals with p<1e-3 flagged as above-95%-CI in the resequencing QQ plot, i.e. suggestive/exploratory)
beta_units: M index scale, targeted resequencing association, additive model
evidence_quote: there are >5 independent low-frequency signals with p < 1e-3 within/near SMARCA2 and VLDLR, with rs7866411 (p = 8.91e-05) and rs2093835 (p = 1.17e-04) being most significantly associated
classification_notes: Paper cites HaploReg-inferred enhancer/DNase peaks in skin (incl. melanocytes/keratinocytes) overlapping tag/LD-linked SNPs (Table S6/S6B), but this is generic epigenomic-annotation overlap, not a demonstrated eQTL/reporter/CRISPR effect on SMARCA2 or VLDLR specifically at this SNP. Separately, paper cites literature-level biological plausibility: SMARCA2 (SWI/SNF component) is recruited by MITF to promoters of TYR/TYRP1/DCT (Vachtenheim et al., 2010 cited); VLDLR knockout mice show hypopigmented retinas (Xia et al., 2013 cited). These are candidate-gene-biology arguments for the GENE, not a demonstrated regulatory link at this SNP. Attempted lookup of rs7866411/rs2093835 in S6B fine-mapping table (region names NOX5/RAB8B/SLC24A5/SMARCA2/SYNE2/TYRP1): rsID NOT found in S6B — the S6B SMARCA2-region credible set (125 rows) contains a different, expanded SNP set (fine-mapping candidates) that does not include this main-text lead SNP by rsID. Honest gap.
source_location: Main text p.1348 (Novel Variants Influence Skin Pigmentation in KhoeSan Populations); Figure 5C

beta_units: M index scale, targeted resequencing association, additive model
evidence_quote: we identify a low frequency association (rs34803545, p = 3.7e-4) ~600 kb upstream of TYRP1. This variant is perfectly linked with multiple conserved variants, one of which exhibits enhancer activity and DNase hypersensitivity specifically in skin
classification_notes: TYRP1 IS on the canonical effector list, so despite the 'near-gene + implied LD' evidence pattern this row is NOT part of the non-canonical candidate set — flagged here as canonical_effector_variant_gap-adjacent but using effector_ambiguous_near status per the strict LD-only evidentiary basis (no demonstrated eQTL/reporter effect on TYRP1 itself, only a HaploReg-annotated skin enhancer/DNase mark on a perfectly-linked partner SNP). Retained in main CSV for completeness but EXCLUDED from martin2017_noncanonical_loci.csv because TYRP1 is canonical. rs34803545 and its cited 'perfectly linked' skin-enhancer partner variant were NOT identifiable by rsID in the S6B fine-mapping table (honest gap).
source_location: Main text p.1348 (Novel Variants Influence Skin Pigmentation in KhoeSan Populations)

gws_threshold: p < 5e-8 (paper explicitly calls this 'suggestive', distinct from the SLC24A5 hit it calls 'genome-wide significant' in the same sentence)
beta_units: M index scale, per derived allele, additive model, phase1+phase2 inverse-variance-weighted meta-analysis (METAL)

evidence_quote: While two tanning status associations met genome-wide significance, none of the loci contained linkage peaks, suggesting that they are most likely spurious, as expected from a phenotype with low heritability.
classification_notes: CRITICAL HONESTY FLAG: paper reports meeting the GWS p-value threshold for tanning status (not baseline pigmentation) but explicitly disclaims the finding as an artifact ('most likely spurious') because no LD/linkage support accompanies the peaks and tanning-status heritability is near zero (Table 1). Retained in this extraction specifically so downstream users do not mistake 'genome_wide_significant=True' for a genuine locus. No rsID, chromosome, position, or gene is named in the source text for these two hits — this is itself the honest-gaps item, not an omission by this extraction.
